title: heracleum pyrenaicum f. setosum
intro: Heracleum pyrenaicum f. setosum, also known as Pyrenean hogweed or hairy hogweed, is a flowering plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Pyrenees mountains. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Heracleum pyrenaicum f. setosum include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of this plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant, as its sap can cause severe skin irritation in some individuals.
